payload.title: Canonical inbox submission still has cross-environment CLI friction
payload.summary: Submitting a Narada inbox proposal from a Windows-hosted Codex session to the WSL Narada proper clone required several environment-discovery attempts. The canonical inbox command worked once invoked directly through Linux Node via nvm, but the obvious pnpm narada path resolved to a Windows-side shim and failed against WSL paths.
evidence: Initial WSL command cd /home/andrey/src/narada && pnpm narada inbox ... failed with MODULE_NOT_FOUND for \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-24.04\home\andrey\src\narada\node_modules\@narada2\cli\dist\main.js.
Direct Windows node against the WSL UNC path failed to resolve package dependencies such as commander.
D:\code\narada had a working older CLI, but it did not expose the canonical inbox command, so it was not the correct target for Narada proper submission.
The successful invocation was: source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh, cd /home/andrey/src/narada, then node packages/layers/cli/dist/main.js inbox submit-observation ...
proposal: Add or document a robust narada doctor/bootstrap path that detects Windows-shim pnpm contamination when operating inside WSL and recommends the correct Linux Node/nvm invocation or repairs the shim.
Provide a canonical cross-environment inbox submission wrapper that can be safely called from Windows Codex sessions targeting a WSL Narada clone without guessing pnpm/node path semantics.

Make inbox submit ergonomics include an explicit read-only preflight showing target repo, CLI build origin, Node runtime origin, and whether the invoked CLI actually contains canonical inbox commands before accepting payload input.
payload.recommendation: Promote to Narada ergonomics task for cross-environment CLI invocation reliability around canonical inbox submission.
